texto: She seemed out of place at the Fairweather. Too posh, as Susan said. Too well dressed. She never strolled along the shore or went bathing or brought a picture postcard. She just sat on the veranda all day with a book she never read, gazing out to sea. Probably wondering why on earth she came here. Susan had said. She looks as if she'd be more at home in Monte Carlo. I know- she's lost all her money gambling and she's waiting for the sea to warm up before she throws herself in. I hope she remembers to pay her bill first.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Vivien Alcock: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “She seemed out of place at the Fairweather. Too posh, as Susan said. Too well dressed. She never …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, inspirational-quote, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e reflexao.

texto: Do you think you could stop crying for a minute? It makes conversation a bit difficult.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Vivien Alcock que provoca temas ligados a motivacao. Ao questionar “Do you think you could stop crying for a minute? It makes conversation a bit difficult.”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre motivacao, crying, inspiracional, com foco em inspiracao e inspiracional.
